title: Lumen — a from-scratch path tracer
description: A physically based path tracer built from scratch in TypeScript with FOUR selectable light-transport integrators: a unidirectional Monte-Carlo path tracer, a full bidirectional path tracer (BDPT) — Veach–Guibas camera×light vertex connections weighted by balance-heuristic multiple importance sampling, which renders indirect-lit scenes (an emitter hidden in a cove) at a fraction of the noise — primary-sample-space Metropolis light transport (PSSMLT), a Markov chain run over the path tracer's own random-number stream (Kelemen mutation + Metropolis-Hastings acceptance, bootstrap normalisation, expected-value splatting, parallel chains across the worker pool) that locks onto the hardest-to-find light and refines the whole frame at once — the path tracer is reused verbatim as the contribution function because the Metropolis sampler simply IS the RNG — AND stochastic progressive photon mapping (SPPM), which runs the transport BACKWARDS to resolve the CAUSTICS the other three sample terribly (light focused through glass onto a floor — a light→specular→diffuse path next-event estimation can't connect through the lens): photons are emitted from the area lights (power-sampled to equal flux), refracted through the specular geometry and deposited at per-pixel measurement points via an exact CSR spatial hash, while a jittered camera pass each iteration places those points and the per-point gather radius is shrunk on the Hachisuka schedule R²←R²·(N+αM)/(N+M) so the density-estimation bias provably vanishes and the estimate converges. Because all four estimators solve the same rendering equation, the in-app verification suite proves they converge to the SAME image (checking PSSMLT and SPPM against the path tracer on both global brightness and spatial distribution), that SPPM resolves a finite focused caustic the others miss, that the photon-gather grid returns exactly the in-radius points vs brute force, and that the MIS weights form an exact partition of unity. The photon mapper is also SPECTRAL — a photon commits a random hero wavelength on its first dispersive hit so the caustic it focuses through glass fans into a RAINBOW (proven to conserve total energy while spreading into colour) — and DAYLIGHT-complete: the sun is itself a photon emitter (photons leave a disc sized to the scene's bounding sphere as a parallel beam), so daylight scenes get photon-mapped SUN CAUSTICS, with the distant-light flux normalisation proven against the path tracer. Showcase scenes include a Caustic Pool — a sine-ripple water surface (analytic smooth normals) throwing the shifting bright filaments of a swimming pool. Plus participating media (fog/smoke/cloud) with Henyey-Greenstein scattering and volumetric god rays, thin-film interference for soap-bubble/oil-slick iridescence, smooth triangle meshes (icosphere, lathe & OBJ import) with barycentric-interpolated normals, a SAH bounding volume hierarchy, GGX microfacet BSDFs with VNDF sampling, smooth and frosted dielectrics, spectral dispersion (prism rainbows), Beer-Lambert volumetric absorption, procedural textures, an analytic Preetham daylight sky whose sun is a sampled light, next-event estimation with multiple importance sampling, scrambled-Halton low-discrepancy sampling, adaptive variance-guided sampling with a live noise heatmap, a multi-threaded Web Worker pool, ACES tone mapping, and an edge-avoiding À-Trous denoiser — global illumination on the CPU, in your browser. The participating media are now HETEROGENEOUS too: procedural 3D fBm density fields (cumulus clouds, rising smoke plumes, exponential ground-fog layers) traced unbiasedly with the null-collision estimators — DELTA TRACKING (Woodcock free-flight sampling against a constant majorant, accepting a real scatter with probability σ(x)/σ̄) for in-volume collisions and RATIO TRACKING for shadow-ray transmittance — so a cloud self-shadows into soft greys, flares a forward-scattering silver lining toward the sun, and breaks light into real volumetric beams, all from one continuously varying field, with five new proofs (delta tracking reproduces e^(−σL), ratio tracking matches a fine quadrature of a varying optical depth, and a heterogeneous pure-scattering volume conserves energy exactly).
